source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Turn end via terminal text
notes: Spec asserts: ready → working → ready when a turn ends with a terminal assistant text message and no tool call (and no pending question). Codex's terminal end signal is a task_complete event_msg on the closing turn; the parser maps it to turn_done, IsAgentDone()=true, and classifier rule 2b fires working→ready. With no tool call the HasOpenToolCall guard never blocks the settle, and with no trailing question cue the classifier never diverts to waiting (the negative invariant on turn_end_terminal). Overlaps intentionally with basic-turn — this cell pins the end-signal mechanism specifically. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver (not headless codex exec): the live REPL stays alive across the one turn so the daemon observes the full working→ready settle on the rollout session, then process_exited only at REPL teardown. The daemon sees the proc-PID pre-session born ready, then the rollout session reconcile (distinct session_id) born ready, then one working/ready flip. turn_end_terminal anchors to turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the earlier birth ready. Final state = ready.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (distinct session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
User prompt drives ready → working on the rollout session
Terminal assistant text with no tool call closes the turn → back to ready; state never entered waiting
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
